docs.text: Jose Renteria Who Am I? I am a Full-Stack Software Engineer with a special interest in User Interface Design and Database Internals. What am I currently obsessed with? Astral — They've got the Midas touch right now, their entire suite of Rust-based tools is pure gold. If you haven't switched to uv yet, you're sleeping. Let uv manage dependencies & virtual environments, ruff for linting, and ty handle your type checking at absurd speeds. Genuinely can't remember what life was like before this, productivity and code quality have increased tenfold. Apache Arrow's PyArrow— I'm usually a heavy Pandas user but PyArrow has kind of stolen the show. The type system is way more rigorous, the in-memory columnar storage is incredibly efficient. It seems Pandas is leaning towards fully adopting PyArrow very soon, but either way it's been a game changer for all tabular work. Apache DataFusion, a query engine written in Rust that I'm definitely obsessed with. When you dig into how it works, you start to appreciate its technical excellence. The gap between "here's what you asked for" and "here's the most efficient way to do it" is where a huge amount of engineering lives — predicate pushdown, projection pruning, join reordering — and DataFusion does all this with a clarity and performance profile that feels like a glimpse at where the next generation of data systems is heading. SQLModel— This one's become my go-to for anything database related. It sits on top of SQLAlchemy and Pydantic which means you get proper data validation basically for free, and writing async APIs that handle database transactions safely feels almost too easy. Less boilerplate, fewer bugs, happier me. Just a glimpse of some of my favorite tools right now.
docs.title: About Me

The gap between "here's what you asked for" and "here's the most efficient way to do it" is where a huge amount of engineering lives — predicate pushdown, projection pruning, join reordering — and DataFusion does all this with a clarity and performance profile that feels like a glimpse at where the next generation of data systems is heading. docs.title: What am I currently obsessed with?

docs.text: Projects JrLytics A mini OLAP database engine built in Python. JrLytics implements segment-based columnar storage with metadata-driven query optimization on top of Apache DataFusion. Github Repository DataFusion Plan Visualizer An interactive web application for visualizing query plans produced by Apache DataFusion. Write SQL against uploaded tables and inspect how DataFusion's query planner decomposes it into a tree of relational operators — across all four plan representations. Live Demo Github Repository LandscAIpe LandscAIpe is an Expo/React Native mobile application for AI-powered landscaping design and contractor matching. Live Now on the app store! It targets iOS and Android, backed by Supabase (PostgreSQL + Edge Functions + Storage) and Google Gemini for image generation. It allows homeowners to: Photograph their yard Select landscaping features and set a budget/vibe Generate AI-powered landscape design images Receive cost estimates Connect with matched local contractors (lead flow) Access a "Yard Nerd" AI chat assistant for plant and gardening Q&A The app targets iOS and Android and is published through the Apple App Store and Google Play Store via Expo EAS. TeqPro A cloud-based technical interview SaaS, with live video conferencing and a built-in compiler that supports over 30 of the most popular programming languages including Python, Javascript, C, and C++. Live Demo Github Repository Auth Master Machine-Generated Text Detection Utility Application using Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) Model. Github Repository Read the Paper PySonic PaaS Grid-Based Python3 code editor for BLV (Blind/Low-Vision) programmers that provides sonic feedback for accessibility and ease of use. Github Repository Dream Job A Cross Platform mobile application that facilitates your dream job search. Github Repository Turing Pattern Generator Python Image Processing project that generates Turing Patterns, built using OpenCV and Numpy. Inspired by Alan Turing's study, "The Chemical Basis of Morphogenesis" Github Repository
